codeReview.summary: The EV integration, modifier alignment, recency smoothing, contact-tier differentiation, and EV breakdown factor are implemented in company_scoring.py. However, the validation for VAL-SCORE-003 is materially weakened in tests/test_ev_scoring.py: the committed test no longer enforces the contract's required top-10%-of-20+-high-value-leads spread assertion and instead checks only 11 graduated leads with top-3 uniqueness. Additional runtime evidence shows high-value synthetic leads still collapse at the ceiling (top scores [100, 100] for 20 leads), so the committed test suite can pass while the contract behavior remains unproven.

issues.description: VAL-SCORE-003 contract mismatch: test_top_leads_not_saturated() no longer validates "top 10% of 20+ synthetic high-value leads". It uses 11 graduated leads and asserts only top-3 non-equality (lines 212-217), which is weaker than the contract requirement in validation-contract.md:155-157. The worker transcript also shows the original 20-lead/top-10% check was changed after failing (worker-transcripts.jsonl, session 6fc46248-6bfc-48cc-af6e-401946088fb1). Action: restore a contract-faithful test using >=20 high-value synthetic leads and top_n=max(2, len(scores)//10), and make the scoring formula/normalization pass that assertion rather than narrowing the test.

Ceiling spread remains insufficient for the contracted VAL-SCORE-003 scenario. With current compression constant MAX_THEORETICAL_ABOVE_BASE=179.0 and integer-rounded final_score, high-value synthetic leads still saturate at the top decile: reproduction on current code yields scores [100,100,...,99] and top 10% of 20 leads = [100,100] (non-distinguishable). This contradicts the intended "top 10% distinguishable" behavior the feature claims to satisfy. Action: adjust normalization/compression and/or output precision so high-value top-decile leads remain distinguishable under the contract dataset, then verify with a matching test.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture knowledge is stale: it still says ev_config.py is "currently unused, being integrated" even though this feature now imports and uses compute_ev in production scoring.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:43 says ev_config is unused; enrichment/company_scoring.py:13 and :362-364 now call compute_ev().

summary: FAIL. Core scoring changes are present, but VAL-SCORE-003 is not reliably satisfied because the committed test was weakened away from the validation contract, and current code still yields non-distinguishable top-decile scores on 20 high-value synthetic leads.
